ModMetadata.Name: Power Grid Plus
ModMetadata.Author: SixFive7
ModMetadata.Description:
[h1]Power Grid Plus[/h1]
Reworks the Stationeers power simulation and turns the three cable tiers into three separate transmission voltages.
A pure-patch mod: no new craftable devices, no asset bundle. The simulation half is derived from Sukasa's Re-Volt (MIT); the three-tier voltage backbone is new. Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames.
This is an early build. The reactive enforcement works; the build-time placement warnings, the transformer tier mapping, and the recipe cost change are still being wired up.
[h2]Features[/h2]
[h3]Reworked power simulation (from Re-Volt)[/h3] [list] [*]Electrical load is shared proportionally across every source on a network, and split proportionally across every load. [*]Cables burn out gradually and probabilistically when overloaded, based on a short rolling average of throughput, instead of instantly. Fuses still blow instantly and always blow before a cable does. [*]Recursive and looped power networks are allowed (the vanilla force-burn check is off by default; re-enable it in settings). [*]Stationary batteries are charge- and discharge-rate limited, with an optional charge-efficiency setting, and expose their rate limits as logic values. [*]Transformers no longer leak free power and charge their own quiescent draw upstream; they expose throughput as the Power Actual logic value. [*]Area Power Controllers no longer leak a small amount of power or slowly drain their battery when nothing downstream is drawing. [/list] Re-Volt's circuit breakers and Load Center are not included; this is the pure-simulation subset.
[h3]Three-tier transmission voltages (new)[/h3] [list] [*]Super-heavy cable never burns out -- it is the long-haul backbone. Normal and heavy cable keep their ratings. [*]Super-heavy cable costs twice as much to craft (configurable; 1.0 = vanilla). Recipe patching still in progress. [*]The three cable tiers are three separate voltages. A cable network must be all one tier; joining two tiers burns the lower-tier cable at the junction and splits the network apart. The only legal bridge is a transformer. [*]Electrical generators belong on heavy cable. A generator on a normal or super-heavy network produces no power. Step generation down through a transformer. [/list]
[h3]Settings[/h3] All settings are server-authoritative: in multiplayer the host's value controls gameplay for everyone. Highlights: [list] [*][b]Cable Burn Factor[/b] (default 1.0): scales the per-tick cable burn chance; 0.0 disables gradual burnout. [*][b]Enable Unlimited Super-Heavy Cables[/b] (default true), [b]Super-Heavy Cable Cost Multiplier[/b] (default 2.0). [*][b]Enable Voltage Tiers[/b] (default true), [b]Generators Require Heavy Cable[/b] (default true). [*][b]Enable Battery Limits[/b] / [b]Max Battery Charge Rate[/b] / [b]Max Battery Discharge Rate[/b] / [b]Battery Charge Efficiency[/b]. [*][b]Enable Transformer Exploit Mitigation[/b], [b]Enable APC Power Fix[/b]. [/list]
[h2]Compatibility[/h2]
[b]Requires:[/b] [list] [*][url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/]BepInEx[/url] [*][url=https://github.com/StationeersLaunchPad/StationeersLaunchPad]StationeersLaunchPad[/url] [/list]
[b]All players[/b] on a server must have Power Grid Plus installed. Matching mod versions are enforced during the connection handshake automatically. [b]Dedicated servers[/b] need the same BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad + Power Grid Plus setup installed server-side.
Re-Volt itself swaps the power tick the same way this mod does, so running both is not supported.
[h2]Reporting Issues[/h2] If you run into a bug or something behaves unexpectedly, please open an issue on [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/issues]GitHub[/url]. Please include the mod name in the title so reports can be triaged. Steam comment notifications don't always come through, so GitHub is the reliable way to make sure a report is seen.
[h2]Credits[/h2] [list] [*]Built on the power-simulation work in [url=https://github.com/sukasa/revolt]Re-Volt[/url] by Sukasa, used under the MIT License. The Re-Volt-derived portions retain their MIT notice; see the repository NOTICE. [/list]
[url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/tree/master/Mods/PowerGridPlus]Source Code on GitHub[/url]
[h2]License[/h2] Licensed under the [url=https://github.com/SixFive7/StationeersPlus/blob/master/LICENSE]Apache License 2.0[/url]. Attribution required; see the LICENSE and NOTICE files at the repository root.
ModMetadata.ChangeLog: v0.1.0: First build (work in progress) - Reworked cable-network power tick from Re-Volt: proportional load sharing, gradual probabilistic cable burnout on a rolling throughput average, NaN-power guard. - Stationary battery charge/discharge-rate limits + optional charge efficiency + rate-limit logic values. - Transformer free-power exploit fix + quiescent-draw restore + Power Actual logic value. - Area Power Controller power-leak / idle-drain fix. - Recursive and looped networks allowed by default. - New: super-heavy cable never burns; the three cable tiers are separate transmission voltages (mixing them burns the boundary cable and splits the network); generators only produce power on heavy cable. - Not finished yet: build-time placement warnings, small/medium/large transformer tier mapping, super-heavy cable coil recipe cost change.
ModMetadata.InGameDescription: Power Grid Plus Reworks the Stationeers power simulation and turns the three cable tiers into three separate transmission voltages. A pure-patch mod (no new prefabs). Simulation half derived from Re-Volt (MIT). Full multiplayer compatibility. Safe to remove from existing savegames. Early build -- some pieces still in progress. From Re-Volt: - Proportional load sharing across sources and loads. - Gradual probabilistic cable burnout on a rolling throughput average; fuses still blow first. - Recursive/looped networks allowed; battery charge/discharge limits + logic values; transformer exploit fix + Power Actual logic value; APC power-leak fix. New: - Super-heavy cable never burns; it is the long-haul backbone. - The three cable tiers are separate voltages; mixing them burns the boundary cable. Bridge tiers with a transformer. - Generators only produce power on heavy cable. Settings: Server-authoritative; the host's values control gameplay for everyone. Requires: BepInEx + StationeersLaunchPad. All players, including dedicated servers, need the same setup.
